techniques.evidence_quote: a zero-day vulnerability of a third-party application was used to gain access to an online asset in a BeyondTrust AWS account
Access to that asset then allowed the threat actor to obtain an infrastructure API key
an infrastructure API key that could then be leveraged against a separate AWS account which operated Remote Support infrastructure
used to enable access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances by resetting local application passwords
infrastructure API key ... leveraged against a separate AWS account
access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances

attack_chain.description: Using access to the Remote Support infrastructure, the attacker reset local application passwords to enable access to 17 customer Remote Support SaaS instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: used to enable access to certain Remote Support SaaS instances by resetting local application passwords

attack_chain.description: Attacker authenticated into customer Remote Support SaaS tenants using the reset local application credentials, affecting 17 customers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: security incident that involved 17 Remote Support SaaS customers

evidence_chunks.quote: security incident that involved 17 Remote Support SaaS customers
security incident that involved 17 Remote Support SaaS customers

initial_access_vector: Zero-day vulnerability in a third-party application exposed on an online asset in a BeyondTrust AWS account (later tracked as CVE-2024-12356 / CVE-2024-12686).
persistence_mechanism: Reset of local application passwords on customer Remote Support SaaS instances to retain access via valid accounts.
privilege_escalation: Stole a BeyondTrust infrastructure API key from the compromised asset and used it to pivot cross-account into the AWS account operating Remote Support infrastructure.
